tags.name: Brownsville Public Utility Board Administration Building
Waelder Substation
tags.note: 138 kV rated equipment operating at 69 kV

tags.name: Horse Hollow 1 & 2 Substation
tags.note: All Horse Hollow 1 and 50 of 130 Horse Hollow 2 turbines are connected here.

tags.name: Horse Hollow Kendall Tie
tags.note: The Horse Hollow wind farms have a point of interconnection at the Bluff Creek Substation and an addition interconnection via a private line to the Kendall Substation. This gives NextEra two prices to sell electricity at.

tags.name: Bluff Creek Substation
tags.note: The Horse Hollow wind farms have a point of interconnection at the Bluff Creek Substation and an addition interconnection via a private line to the Kendall Substation. This gives NextEra two prices to sell electricity at.

tags.name: Bear Grass Substation
tags.note: The lines interconnected here supply power for the mine excavators

tags.note: 80 of 130 Horse Hollow 2 turbines are connected here

tags.note: Former Xcel substation sold to LP&L. Xcel still owns the transmission busses and switchgear. LP&L owns the substation property, transformers and distribution equipment.

tags.name: Colorado River Municipal Water District #1 Substation
West Childress Substation
tags.note: There are two sets of 138 kV and 69 kV busses in this substation. One set is owned by AEP TNC and connected to ERCOT; the other AEP SWEPCO connected to SPP. A 69 kV breaker connects the two for emergency transfers.
